title: Arthromeris proteus
intro: Arthromeris proteus, also known as the Proteus fern, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, improve wound healing, and protect against infections. The bioactive constituents of Arthromeris proteus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Arthromeris proteus is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used in the form of a topical paste or salve, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
taxonomy: Arthromeris proteus is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly known as the polypody ferns. This family is part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. Within the Polypodiaceae family, Arthromeris proteus is likely placed in a specific genus or subgenus, though further classification may be needed for a precise identification. The genus Arthromeris itself is likely part of a larger group such as the subfamily Polypodioideae or the superfamily Polypodioideae. Further research may provide a more detailed classification for Arthromeris proteus.
